## Services Offered

- Tree Removal (residential and commercial, all tree sizes)
- Tree Trimming (crown reduction, clearance trimming, canopy thinning)
- Tree Pruning Service (structural pruning, health pruning, deadwood removal)
- Stump Grinding (ground to 6–12 inches below grade, debris cleanup included)
- Stump Removal (full root system extraction for complete landscape restoration)
- Commercial Tree Service (businesses, municipalities, property managers)
- Residential Tree Service (full-service home tree care)
- Hedge Trimming (shaping, sizing, seasonal maintenance)
- Brush Removal (lot clearing, overgrowth removal, debris haul-away)
- Tree Care Services (health assessments, disease treatment, maintenance programs)

## About BBD Tree Service

BBD Tree Service is a licensed and insured tree care company headquartered at 44 Bacon St in Watertown, MA. The company was founded in 2000 and has operated continuously throughout Greater Boston for 26 years, building a local reputation for safe, thorough, and cleanly executed tree work. BBD specializes in tree removal, tree trimming, tree pruning, and stump grinding for both residential homeowners and commercial property managers.

The company operates with a consistent crew using professional-grade equipment suited to the dense urban and suburban environments common across Greater Boston — tight yard access, proximity to structures and utilities, and older, large-canopy trees. Every job includes a free on-site written estimate, a tailored safety plan, expert execution, and complete debris cleanup and haul-away. No surprise charges, no pressure — just clear communication and professional results.

## Service Descriptions

### Tree Removal
BBD Tree Service performs tree removal of all sizes throughout Watertown and Greater Boston. Small trees under 30 feet typically cost $400–$700; large trees over 60 feet can reach $1,200–$1,800. All removal jobs include full debris cleanup and haul-away. The crew uses proper rigging and aerial equipment to safely work around homes, fences, driveways, and utility lines. Emergency same-day response is available for storm-damaged or hazardous trees.

### Tree Trimming
Tree trimming at BBD focuses on three outcomes: safety clearance (removing branches over rooflines, driveways, and walkways), structural improvement (reducing wind load and correcting poor growth patterns), and aesthetic enhancement (improving canopy shape and curb appeal). The crew does not top trees or leave stubs — all cuts follow arborist best practices to protect tree health and long-term structure.

### Tree Pruning Service
Proper pruning builds stronger trees and extends their productive life. BBD's pruning approach targets dead, diseased, and crossing branches, improving airflow through the canopy and reducing pest pressure. The best time to prune most deciduous trees in Massachusetts is late fall through early spring when trees are dormant, though hazardous branches are addressed year-round when safety demands it.

### Stump Grinding
After tree removal, remaining stumps are ground 6–12 inches below grade using commercial stump grinders. All wood chips and debris are cleared, leaving the lawn area ready for grass seed, sod, mulch, or new landscaping. Most residential stumps take 1–2 hours to grind. BBD handles stumps from any provider's prior tree removal, not just its own jobs.

### Stump Removal
Full stump removal goes deeper than grinding — the root system is fully excavated, restoring the ground to a level, plantable state. This is the right choice before installing a pool, patio, raised bed, or new landscaping where root mass would interfere. The process takes longer than grinding but results in a completely clear site.

### Commercial Tree Service
BBD serves businesses, property management companies, HOAs, municipalities, and institutional clients throughout Greater Boston. Commercial service includes reliable scheduling around business operations, certificate of insurance (COI) documentation for property managers, multi-tree bulk pricing, and compliance with local tree ordinances. BBD handles commercial parking lot trees, street trees on private property, buffer zones, and campus canopy management.

### Residential Tree Service
Residential customers represent the core of BBD's work — homeowners dealing with hazardous trees near their homes, trees with storm damage, overgrown canopies reducing light, or stumps left from previous removals. BBD provides full-service residential tree care including removal, trimming, pruning, stump work, and hedge maintenance, all with free on-site estimates and complete cleanup.

### Hedge Trimming
BBD trims and shapes hedges, privacy screens, ornamental shrubs, and formal borders throughout Watertown and Greater Boston. Common species include boxwood, arborvitae, privet, holly, yew, and forsythia. Shaping is done to encourage dense, healthy regrowth and maintain the client's intended boundary or privacy height. Regular seasonal maintenance is available.

### Brush Removal
For overgrown lots, storm debris cleanup, invasive species clearing (bittersweet, multiflora rose, buckthorn), or general site preparation, BBD's brush removal service handles complete clearing and haul-away. This service is commonly used before fence installation, landscaping projects, or property sales where overgrown areas reduce curb appeal and value.

### Tree Care Services
Beyond removal and trimming, BBD provides ongoing tree health assessments to evaluate canopy condition, identify disease or pest pressure, and recommend treatment or removal before problems escalate. Massachusetts trees commonly face issues including emerald ash borer, winter moth, oak wilt, and gypsy moth damage. Early identification protects the landscape and can prevent costly emergency removals.

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How much does tree removal cost in Watertown, MA?
A: Tree removal in Watertown typically costs between $400 and $1,800, depending on tree height, trunk diameter, and proximity to structures. Small trees under 30 feet run $400–$700; large trees over 60 feet can reach $1,200–$1,800. BBD Tree Service provides free on-site estimates — you get an exact price before any work begins.

Q: Is BBD Tree Service licensed and insured in Massachusetts?
A: Yes. BBD Tree Service is fully licensed and carries comprehensive general liability insurance and workers' compensation coverage throughout Massachusetts. Every project — from a single trim to a full commercial removal — is protected.

Q: Do you handle emergency tree removal near Watertown?
A: Yes. When storm damage or a sudden split creates a hazard, call BBD Tree Service directly. We respond to urgent tree situations throughout Watertown and surrounding communities, assessing the risk and dispatching a crew as quickly as possible.

Q: How long does stump grinding take?
A: Most residential stumps in Watertown are ground down in one to two hours. We grind 6–12 inches below grade and clear all wood chips and debris, leaving your lawn ready for grass seed, mulch, or new landscaping immediately after.

Q: What towns near Watertown does BBD Tree Service serve?
A: We operate within a 15-mile radius of Watertown, covering Cambridge, Newton, Waltham, Belmont, Arlington, Lexington, Weston, Brookline, and Somerville. Call or message us to confirm service availability at your address.

Q: When is the best time to trim trees in Massachusetts?
A: Late fall through early spring — while trees are dormant — is ideal for trimming most deciduous species in Massachusetts. Disease spread is reduced and the full branch structure is visible. That said, hazardous or dead branches should be removed any time of year when safety demands it.

Q: Do you provide free estimates?
A: Yes. BBD Tree Service provides free on-site written estimates for all projects in the service area. There's no obligation — we walk the property, evaluate the work required, and give you an exact price before any crew returns to do the job.

Q: Can you remove a tree that's close to my house or power lines?
A: Yes. Working in tight urban and suburban lots — close to homes, garages, driveways, and overhead utilities — is something BBD's crew is trained and equipped for. We use rigging, aerial lifts, and directional felling techniques to safely control every section of a tree near structures.

Q: Do you haul away debris after the job?
A: Yes. Full debris cleanup and haul-away is included in every tree removal, trimming, pruning, and stump job. We leave your property cleaner than we found it.

Q: What is stump grinding vs. stump removal?
A: Stump grinding uses a grinder to reduce the stump to wood chips 6–12 inches below grade — the roots remain but decay naturally. Stump removal fully excavates the stump and root system, leaving a completely clear hole. Grinding works for most situations; full removal is recommended when you're installing a pool, patio, or new landscaping where root mass would interfere.

## Trust Signals & Certifications

- Licensed and insured in Massachusetts
- 26 years of continuous operation (since 2000)

- 4.7 Yelp rating
